duration.notes: Start inferred from earliest A-output file write time (A0). End set to R1 completion date.

what_went_well: Core validation capabilities were delivered across linter, drift detection, migration compatibility, and CI workflow areas.
Automated quality signal improved through unit/integration coverage and explicit protocol-rule test cases (WP001-WP013).
Coverage exceeded threshold (lines/statements 75.71%, branches 66.55%, functions 71.84% vs 60% target).
Drift detector and migration suites passed in focused verification runs, including non-destructive migration checks on fixtures.
Final integration gate (A6) consolidated AC-level evidence and documented concrete remediation steps for remaining repository gates.
what_could_be_improved: Repository-wide quality gates were not green at merge verification time due to pre-existing TypeScript and ESLint issues outside narrow implementation scope.
Validation tests required temporary execution/config workarounds in some scenarios (e.g., focused configs), increasing verification friction.
Workpack-wide lint-all still surfaced protocol inconsistencies (errors/warnings) across sibling instances, indicating uneven protocol hygiene.
Output metadata consistency varied across prompts (mixed timestamp precision and occasional missing execution/prompt fields), reducing metric reliability for retrospectives.

quality_observations.linter_real_issues: Yes. Protocol linter checks and scoped lint-all surfaced actionable protocol and schema issues; repository ESLint also surfaced real pre-existing issues.
quality_observations.drift_detection_useful: Yes. Drift detection behavior was validated with targeted fixtures and expected-drift/no-drift paths, improving confidence in state/artifact reconciliation.
quality_observations.migration_tool_v5_edges: Mostly yes. v5->v6 migration tests passed including non-destructive behavior and schema-valid generation, but full-repo confidence remains contingent on clearing unrelated global TypeScript blockers.
quality_observations.coverage_thresholds_appropriate: Yes for current stage. 60% threshold was comfortably exceeded, though raising thresholds incrementally after baseline cleanup would improve long-term quality pressure.

project_wide_retrospective.protocol_v6_experience: Overall positive: the v6 protocol enabled structured prompts, explicit outputs, and clearer acceptance evidence. Main pain point was cross-workpack consistency drift over time.
project_wide_retrospective.dependency_graph_effectiveness: Effective at sequencing dependencies (e.g., A6 relying on prior prompt outputs), though final verification still depended on pre-existing repository health that sat outside workpack-local scope.
project_wide_retrospective.workpack_boundary_fit: Mostly appropriate. Validation quality tasks were coherent, but repo-level quality debt (lint/tsc) crossed boundaries and affected gate outcomes.
recommendations: Add an early shared baseline-cleanup phase for global type/lint debt before feature workpacks begin.
Standardize output JSON metadata fields (timestamps, execution block, prompt identifiers) to improve retrospective and dashboard automation.
Keep a periodic cross-workpack lint-all checkpoint to catch protocol drift earlier.
Introduce explicit gate taxonomy (workpack-local vs repo-global) so caveats are classified consistently during handoff.

metrics.average_prompt_duration: ~3m05s observed across A0-A6 output write-time window (artifact-based approximation)
metrics.drift_issues_found: 0 confirmed in production workpack artifacts; drift scenarios validated via dedicated fixture tests

action_items.action: Fix the pre-existing TypeScript signature issue in src/__mocks__/__tests__/vscode-mock.test.ts to restore global tsc gate.
Resolve top-priority ESLint violations blocking CI merge checks.
Run and fix protocol lint-all issues across workpack-manager instances (errors first, then warnings).
Normalize output metadata schema usage across A/R prompts for reliable retrospective automation.
